hero.title: We build the digital infrastructure that <accent>moves Africa forward</accent>.
hero.subtitle: Impact Solutions designs, develops and deploys high-impact digital platforms for governments, international institutions and businesses transforming the continent.

mission.paragraph1: We are a pan-African digital engineering company. Our mission: accelerate Africa's digital transformation through technology solutions that address real development challenges.
mission.paragraph2: From digital health to digital governance, artificial intelligence and information systems, we design platforms that are robust, interoperable and built to scale.
mission.paragraph3: Our products — KEREYA, PASSBI, AFRIASSESS — prove this capability: solutions deployed in the field, serving populations and the institutions that support them.

expertiseSection.title: Full-stack engineering, from data to infrastructure

solutionsSection.title: Digital solutions designed for impact
solutionsSection.description: We build platforms that address key sector challenges and durably improve people's lives.

cta.title: Let's talk about your next digital project.
cta.subtitle: Institutions, funders, businesses: our team supports you from scoping to deployment.

hero.title: Digital engineering built for Africa
hero.description: We design platforms that transform healthcare, mobility, and governance across the continent — with the rigor of a software company and the grounding of a field team.

history.title: Our story
history.paragraph1: Impact Solutions was founded on a conviction: Africa doesn't need imported solutions, but digital engineering built from the continent, for its own realities.
history.paragraph2: Based in Dakar, our team brings together engineers, data scientists, and designers who grew up with the constraints they solve: intermittent connectivity, heterogeneous systems, and demanding institutional requirements.
history.paragraph3: Today, our platforms — KEREYA, PassBi, AFRIASSESS, JOKO AI, and AMANET — are deployed in Senegal, Guinea, and soon across the WAEMU zone, serving ministries, international funders, and private operators.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.body: Design and deploy digital solutions that durably improve essential services in Africa — healthcare, mobility, environment, governance. Every line of code we write must produce a measurable impact in the field.

vision.title: Our vision
vision.body: Become the pan-African reference in impact-driven digital engineering: a company whose platforms are recognized by institutions, funders, and industrial partners as the standard of technical excellence on the continent.

values.title: Our values
values.subtitle: Seven principles that guide every decision, every line of code, every deployment.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.body: We continuously explore new approaches — AI, offline-first, interoperability — to solve problems that conventional solutions overlook.

excellence.title: Excellence
excellence.body: Technical rigor at every stage: architecture built for scale, automated testing, systematic code reviews.

impact.title: Impact
impact.body: Every solution must deliver a measurable effect. No technology for technology's sake — results for people.

reliability.title: Reliability
reliability.body: Our platforms work when there's no stable network or constant power. Robustness isn't a bonus, it's a prerequisite.

openness.title: Openness
openness.body: Open standards, documented APIs, native interoperability. We build bridges, not silos.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.body: We work alongside our partners, not just for them. Co-design, skills transfer, local ownership.

ethics.title: Ethics
ethics.body: Data protection, explainable AI, informed consent. Responsible technology is the only sustainable technology.

approach.title: Our approach
approach.subtitle: From field understanding to production deployment, a proven five-step methodology.

s1.title: Scoping & immersion
s1.body: Field workshops with business teams to understand real constraints, formalize needs, and define success indicators.

s2.title: Product design
s2.body: Interactive prototypes, user testing, and scope trade-offs before any code is written. We validate with end users, not in meeting rooms.

s3.title: Engineering
s3.body: Iterative development, architecture built for scale, automated testing, and continuous integration. Systematic code review.

s4.title: Deployment
s4.body: Controlled production rollout, field team training, and skills transfer for lasting ownership.

s5.title: Run & evolution
s5.body: Continuous monitoring, responsive support, improvement cycles, and a long-term roadmap co-built with our partners.

team.title: Engineers who know the field
team.body: Our team combines high-level technical expertise with a deep understanding of African contexts. Software engineers, data scientists, GIS specialists, UX designers — every profile is chosen for their ability to deliver solutions that work in the real conditions of the continent.

meta.title: Our Expertise
meta.description: Discover our areas of expertise: AI, web and mobile development, cloud, cybersecurity, data, digital health and more. IMPACT SOLUTIONS drives Africa's digital transformation.

c1.problem: High maternal mortality and paper-based patient tracking in rural districts.
c1.solution: KEREYA digital maternal health platform with offline-first architecture.
c1.result: Continuous tracking of thousands of pregnant women, timestamped digital referral between care levels.

c2.problem: Siloed transport networks, no shared ticketing or multimodal planning.
c2.solution: PassBi platform with RAPTOR routing, NFC ticketing and mobile payments.
c2.result: 292 NFC-equipped buses, official partnership for YOG Dakar 2026.

c3.problem: 3-week impact report production, data in incompatible silos.
c3.solution: AFRIASSESS: automated harmonization and AI generation of donor-compliant reports.
c3.result: RAP report produced in 1 day instead of 3 weeks.

c4.problem: Manual Excel management, 2-5 day customs clearance, 15-20% error rate.
c4.solution: AMANET: 11-module ERP with digitized customs and ML-suggested HS codes.

t1.name: Dr. Amadou Diallo
t1.quote: IMPACT SOLUTIONS transformed how we track pregnancies in rural districts. The KEREYA platform works even without network, which changes everything for our health workers.

t2.name: Fatou Ndiaye
t2.quote: PassBi allowed us to connect our network to all of Dakar's urban transport. The team understands our constraints and delivers with exemplary rigor.

t3.name: Moussa Camara
t3.quote: With AFRIASSESS, we produce in one day what used to take three weeks. Donor compliance is automatic and our clients are impressed.

q1.question: What types of projects do you deliver?
q1.answer: We deliver projects ranging from web and mobile applications to complex SaaS platforms, including health information systems, intelligent transport solutions, business ERPs and artificial intelligence projects. Each solution is custom-built to address your specific challenges.

q2.question: Do you work across Africa?
q2.answer: Yes, we support clients in 12 African countries. Our headquarters are in Dakar, and we primarily operate in West Africa (Senegal, Guinea, Côte d'Ivoire, Mali, Benin, Togo) with expansion into Central and East Africa.

q3.question: What technologies do you use?
q3.answer: We master a wide technology spectrum: Go, Node.js, Python, React, Next.js, Flutter for development; AWS, Azure, GCP for cloud; PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis for databases; and the latest AI models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama) for artificial intelligence.

q4.question: How long does a project take?
q4.answer: Duration depends on complexity: an MVP can be delivered in 6-8 weeks, a full platform in 3-6 months. We favor iterative delivery so you can see initial results quickly and validate direction before each phase.

q5.question: How does a collaboration start?
q5.answer: Everything starts with a conversation to understand your needs. We then organize a scoping workshop to formalize scope, objectives and success metrics. A detailed quote is provided with a provisional timeline. Once approved, we start with a discovery sprint.

hero.title: Platforms built for impact, proven in the field
hero.description: Our products support ministries, agencies and donors deploying essential services at scale. Every solution is architected for interoperability, compliance and continuous operation.

overview.title: Five platforms, one engineering standard
overview.description: KEREYA, PASSBI, AFRIASSESS, JOKO AI and AMANET are products deployed in the field. Our custom solutions extend these building blocks to address specific needs.

kereya.name: KEREYA
kereya.body: KEREYA tackles the three delays in maternal and child health — from the decision to seek care to hospital referral — in rural health districts across Senegal.
kereya.pitch: A digital platform to reduce the three delays in maternal, neonatal and child health — from the decision to seek care to hospital referral, designed for rural health districts in Senegal.
problem: Senegal records a high maternal mortality rate (315 per 100,000 births), particularly critical in rural areas such as Fouladou (Kolda medical region).
Existing systems like DHIS2 do not digitize clinical registers: they only transmit aggregated reports to the Ministry, with no individual digital tracking of patients.
Pregnant women and children are monitored through paper registers and physical health booklets, exposed to loss, illegibility and care continuity gaps between levels of care.
Referrals between health posts, districts and hospitals are handled informally, with no traceability or feedback (counter-referral) to the originating facility.

value.title: Integrated three-delays platform
value.body: 11 sub-modules covering the decision to seek care, clinical management (ANC, PNC, EPI, family planning) and timestamped digital referral between care levels.

value.title: Offline-first architecture
value.body: Works without stable connectivity, with deferred synchronization adapted to remote rural areas and seasonal rainy-period constraints.

value.title: Community mobilization in the field
value.body: Dedicated tools for community health workers (Bajenu Gox, Community Health Agents) for grassroots awareness-raising and Universal Health Coverage enrollment.

value.title: Built-in scientific evaluation
value.body: A matched Difference-in-Differences quasi-experimental design, developed with an independent evaluation firm to rigorously measure project impact.

impact.body: ANC, delivery and postnatal care united in a single digital record, from first to last contact.
Automatic alerts for antenatal consultations and child vaccinations.
Real-time indicator dashboard, interoperable with the national DHIS2.
Every transfer between facilities is timestamped, notified and documented through to effective care delivery.

passbi.name: PassBi
passbi.body: PassBi connects BRT, TER, Dem Dikk and AFTU in a single app — NFC ticketing, smart routing and real-time mobility data.
passbi.pitch: The SaaS multimodal urban-mobility platform that connects BRT, TER, Dem Dikk and AFTU in a single app — NFC ticketing, intelligent trip planning and real-time mobility data.
problem: Dakar's public transport networks (BRT SunuBRT, TER, Dem Dikk, AFTU) operate in silos, with no shared ticketing or trip planning.
Riders juggle multiple tickets, multiple queues and have no visibility on multimodal transfers.
Transport operators have no unified dashboard to steer ridership, revenue and line performance.
As the Dakar 2026 Youth Olympic Games approach, no unified digital solution exists to guide visitors, athletes and delegations across the network.

value.title: Unified multimodal platform
value.body: RAPTOR routing engine on GTFS data, optimized time-and-transfer routing across the full BRT / TER / Dem Dikk / AFTU network.

value.title: Integrated NFC ticketing
value.body: 292 AFTU buses already equipped with NFC validators — one medium to validate any trip, no paper ticket.

value.title: Wallet & mobile payments
value.body: Top-up and payment via Wave, Orange Money (BCEAO framework).

value.title: Mobility data & intelligence
value.body: CO₂ dashboard per trip, real-time ridership data for operators and transport authorities.

impact.body: No more multiple tickets: a multimodal trip planned and paid in a single app.
Ridership, revenue and line-performance dashboard, always on.
Official partnership with Dem Dikk for Youth Olympic Games transport — visibility and data governance for authorities.
Built in Dakar, designed for regional replication: Côte d'Ivoire, Benin, Togo, Guinea.

afriassess.name: AFRIASSESS
afriassess.body: AFRIASSESS structures, centralizes and streamlines the evaluation of environmental and social project impacts.
afriassess.pitch: The entire impact-study chain — RAP, ESIA, ESMP — industrialized on a single platform aligned with donor requirements. From field data to final report in one day instead of three weeks.
problem: Field data arrives in incompatible silos (KoboCollect CSV, KMZ / QGIS, commission Excel registers) — reconciling it cleanly takes days.
Report drafting is time-consuming: up to three weeks for a standard RAP.
Compensation calculations in Excel are not auditable, generating disputes with affected people.
Every donor (World Bank, AFD, AfDB, IFC) imposes its own formats and standards, forcing each deliverable to be reworked.

value.title: Intelligent Harmonizer
value.body: Multi-source import (CSV, XLSX, KMZ, GeoJSON) and automated merging of socio-economic and cartographic data in 20 minutes instead of days.

value.title: AI report, donor-compliant
value.body: AI-assisted generation of a structured report — 10 sections for a RAP — with systematic citation of sources (standards, scales, legislation).

value.title: Mapping & GIS
value.body: Site localization, environmental datasets, risk exposure and identification of sensitive areas.

value.title: Compliance & traceability
value.body: Templates aligned with World Bank (ESS 5 / OP 4.12), IFC (PS5), AfDB and AFD — with a traceable validation circuit between consultant, commission and donor.

innovation.title: 4-layer matching engine
innovation.body: Deterministic normalization, multi-criteria scoring (Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein), AI pattern detection that learns from corrections, and assisted human resolution. Reconciles incompatible identifiers across parties.

innovation.title: Africa-contextualized RAG
innovation.body: A corpus of standards, official scales and legislation per country, human-validated before indexing — never fed raw from the internet. Every report stays anchored in the real legal framework of the country concerned.

innovation.title: Traceable AI report
innovation.body: Every generated section cites its sources — version of the scale, effective date, official reference. Legal traceability is preserved for donor due diligence.

impact.body: Compliant templates and automated harmonization radically accelerate deliverable production.
Files are reviewed, annotated and validated in a centralized workspace.
Direct access to history, versions and compliance evidence — with cited sources.
Studies are accessible to the public, with a structured feedback space.

amanet.name: AMANET
amanet.body: AMANET industrializes the operational chain of freight-forwarding agencies — quotes, fleet, customs, invoicing and HR — on a single SYSCOHADA-compliant platform.
amanet.pitch: The full operational chain of logistics transit — quotes, fleet, customs, invoicing, HR — industrialized on a single SYSCOHADA-compliant platform.
problem: Manual operations via Excel and email, with no real-time visibility on vehicles or drivers.
Manual customs clearance: two to five days of waiting, paper files that can't be processed.
Paper invoicing, payment delays, and a 15–20% error rate on files.
Payroll, HR and customer data managed manually, without structured tracking or traceability.

value.title: 11-module ERP platform
value.body: Quotes, fleet, customs, invoicing, HR wired together — each datum entered once flows through the whole system.

value.title: Real-time fleet tracking
value.body: Geolocation of 25–35 vehicles and 30–40 drivers, fuel and maintenance tracking.

value.title: Digitized customs clearance
value.body: ASYCUDA++ workflow with ML-suggested HS code (80% accuracy) and automated RFI handling.

value.title: SYSCOHADA compliance
value.body: Automated invoicing, accounting and Senegalese payroll (IPRES, CSS, IRPP, 18% VAT) aligned with local standards.

impact.body: A file moves from 30–40 days to 5–7 days.
Versus two to five days on the manual procedure.
Generated on delivery confirmation, errors below 2%.
Versus two to three days of manual processing previously.

custom.body: We design custom digital platforms to address our partners' specific challenges, from scoping to deployment.
custom.pitch: When no existing building block fits, we design the platform that matches your context exactly — from the first line of code to production run.

jokoai.name: JOKO AI
jokoai.body: JOKO AI is the operating system for African startups — activatable modules, AI-powered, calibrated for local reality: OHADA accounting, CFA francs, mobile money.
jokoai.pitch: The operating system for African startups — from idea to IPO. Activatable modules, AI-powered, calibrated for local reality: OHADA accounting, CFA francs, mobile money.
problem: African founders juggle dozens of scattered tools (Excel, WhatsApp, PDF) with no consolidated view of their startup.
Western SaaS tools ignore local reality: OHADA chart of accounts, CFA francs (XOF), mobile money (Wave, Orange Money).
Structured support — finance, runway, pitch, governance — remains reserved for an elite already connected to incubators and VCs.
Without financial and operational steering, most startups shut down before proving their model.

value.title: Activatable modules
value.body: Invoicing, OHADA Accounting, Treasury & Runway, Roadmap, Pitch Studio — activate the building blocks you need, Odoo-style.

value.title: AI agents by role
value.body: Co-Founder AI, CFO AI, CTO AI and YC Senior Coach analyze your real data and advise you continuously.

value.title: Calibrated for Africa
value.body: Native OHADA chart of accounts, XOF, mobile money integration, interface optimized for limited bandwidth.

value.title: Event-driven architecture
value.body: Multi-tenant and multi-venture: data entered once feeds the entire system, every module communicates with the others.

innovation.title: Centralized AI gateway
innovation.body: All LLM calls go through an AiGateway: semantic cache, controlled costs, outputs validated by Zod.

innovation.title: Specialized agents
innovation.body: Versioned system prompts, explicitly declared tools — one agent per hat: Co-Founder, CFO, CTO, YC Coach.

innovation.title: Long-term memory
innovation.body: pgvector preserves the context of each venture for advice that sharpens over time.

impact.body: Treasury, burn rate and survival alerts automatically computed from your financial data.
An investor pitch deck generated from your real numbers, ready to present and exportable.
The health of every incubated startup tracked from a single dashboard.
Structured startups with reliable metrics and an Investor Readiness Score.
